cases.description: parseVerdict correctly extracts an ACCEPT decision from a clean reviewer response
cases.detail: decision: got "accept", want "accept" → OK | nonNegotiable: got false, want false → OK

cases.description: parseVerdict correctly detects a NON-NEGOTIABLE rejection when a hardcoded secret is present
cases.detail: decision: got "reject", want "reject" → OK | nonNegotiable: got true, want true → OK | violatedRule contains "hardcoded": "No hardcoded secrets, API keys, or client_ids in source code" → OK

cases.description: parseVerdict extracts a negotiable REJECT (e.g. naming convention) and sets nonNegotiable to false
cases.detail: decision: got "reject", want "reject" → OK | nonNegotiable: got false, want false → OK

cases.description: parseVerdict handles JSON wrapped in markdown code fences (common LLM output pattern)
cases.detail: decision: got "accept", want "accept" → OK | nonNegotiable: got false, want false → OK

cases.description: parseVerdict returns a synthetic reject verdict when the agent response contains no valid JSON — prevents the pipeline from hanging
cases.detail: decision: got "reject", want "reject" → OK | reason starts with "Could not parse": "Could not parse reviewer response: I looked at the code. It seems fine to me. No" → OK

cases.description: parseTestResult correctly identifies a PASS when the agent outputs 'ok' and ends with PASS
cases.detail: passed: got true, want true → OK | failures.length: got 0, want 0 → OK

cases.description: parseTestResult correctly identifies a FAIL when go test output contains '--- FAIL'
cases.detail: passed: got false, want false → OK | failures non-empty: 2 failure(s) → OK

cases.description: parseTestResult treats 'no test files' as a pass — not every package has tests
cases.detail: passed: got true, want true → OK

cases.description: Pipeline convergence logic: a pass+accept in round 1 immediately sets status to 'merged'
cases.detail: finalStatus: got "merged", want "merged" → OK | roundsConsumed: got 1, want 1 → OK

cases.description: Pipeline convergence logic: a non-negotiable reject on round 1 sets status to 'unresolved' and stops immediately
cases.detail: finalStatus: got "unresolved", want "unresolved" → OK | roundsConsumed: got 1, want 1 → OK

cases.description: Pipeline convergence logic: repeated negotiable rejects exhaust MAX_ROUNDS and result in 'unresolved'
cases.detail: finalStatus: got "unresolved", want "unresolved" → OK | roundsConsumed: got 5, want 5 → OK

cases.description: Pipeline convergence logic: a test failure on round 1 followed by pass+accept on round 2 results in 'merged'
cases.detail: finalStatus: got "merged", want "merged" → OK | roundsConsumed: got 2, want 2 → OK
